metaTitle: Harmonic Study and Analysis in Australia for Companies & Businesses
metaDescription: Carelabs offers professional harmonic study and analysis for companies across Australia ensure that your property meets safety standards.
h1: HARMONIC STUDY AND ANALYSIS IN AUSTRALIA
h2: Carelabs Work Process of Harmonic Study and Analysis:
Where Proactive Safety Meets Power Engineering
paragraphs: Carelabs provides several services for electrical installations such as electrical safety inspection, testing, calibration, and certification services and we offer comprehensive harmonic study and analysis services.
Trained professionals will examine harmonic risks, training programs, documentation, and, PPE to ensure your electrical safety program is up to date, compliant, and comprehensive.
Carelabs are capable of identifying harmonics at an early stage and can adopt appropriate steps to mitigate the effects of harmonic distortions to ensure the safe operation of the equipment.
Harmonics are electric voltages and currents that can introduce inefficiencies in the electrical system. They cause wasteful heat and can cause power quality problems.
Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical approach in predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels depending on the available data.
Harmonic frequencies enter the electrical system through nonlinear electrical switching devices like AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, computers, etc.
There is a high chance of equipment malfunction during high harmonic voltage/current levels. so, a timely harmonic study and analysis are important.
Present harmonic limits in 415 voltage systems are 5% which includes 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics.

The possibility of occurrence of harmonic problems are very less but they tend to reduce power system reliability and level of power quality and its efficiency on appearing. So harmonic study and analysis is essential as it

Harmonic study and analysis will provide an insight into the potential cause & effect of power quality issues and will help in avoiding harmonic-related issues in the design stage itself and decrease the possibility of undesired effects.

Harmonic due to large installations are included in the standard AS/NZS 61000.3.6 with effect from 1999. The scope of this standard is to give guidance in determining the size of the distorting equipment that may be supplied from high voltage or the voltage at the PCC is greater than 240/415V.
Low and high voltages according to IEC terms are (in terms of line-to-line voltage)
AS2279.2 allows harmonic analysis in three stages. Stage 1 involves a quick assessment of the ratio of converter rating to the fault level at PCC.
Stage 2 involves higher ratings if the existing background harmonics have been measured.

Carelabs have their crew members deployed in various locations, so our specialists are nearby in case of a routine or emergency. In a very short period, Carelabs have been established as an ISO 9001:2008 certified organization and has acquired a reputed client base with excellent feedbacks.
We offer harmonic study and analysis services in all the major cities of New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania, Victoria and Western Australia.
Book your harmonic study and analysis in Australia or get a quote today by contacting us!
From fault analysis to electrical safety compliance, we have the right solution for you at Carelabs.

Equipment malfunction and destruction due to excessive voltage distortion

Explosion and fires in wiring and distribution system
Downtime and unnecessary tripping of drives
Improve power factor analysis and maintain value close to one
Highlight available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and medium voltage cables of long length from the electrical one-line diagram
Identify the place of common coupling (PCC)
Identify in-plant system buses which are prone to be affected by harmonic distortions
Obtain harmonics-related data from all nonlinear loads
Obtain important data of voltage and current harmonics at the predetermined PCC including highest and lowest short circuit fault levels and the permitted harmonic levels of Australia
Perform harmonic analysis of the electrical network using ETAP
Obtain both the whole and discrete levels of current and voltage wave distortions at the buses and the PCC
if the harmonic distortion levels exceed the permitted levels, a suitable mitigation solution is proposed and applied
Reperform the analysis after the addition of mitigation solution
If the background is below 25%, stage 2 allows converter size depending on fault level and converter type.
If the background is between 25% and 75%, converter ratings of half that given above point are allowed.
For higher background, a full harmonic investigation is done.
